items.title: Agentic Vulnerability Analysis in AI Agents (Project Leader)
items.focus: National broadcast collaboration on LLM agent vulnerability analysis.
highlights: Led a 4-person team to analyze real-world vulnerabilities of LLM agents featured in a national broadcast.
Conducted indirect prompt injection evaluation, identifying up to 55% attack success rate in GPT-5.2.
Developed the PnP-XAI app for interpretable LLM behavior analysis and demonstration.
Built and analyzed OpenClaw and Moltbook, revealing failures in multi-agent environments.

items.title: Visiting Research Scholar
items.focus: Mechanistic interpretability and bias in LLMs.
highlights: Conducted research at New York University's Global AI Frontier Lab.
Investigated bias representation in large language models through neuron- and circuit-level analysis.
Collaborated on interpretability frameworks for scalable model analysis.

items.title: Teaching Assistant
items.focus: Time Series, Deep Learning, and AI Colloquium.
highlights: Served as a teaching assistant for graduate-level courses in time series, deep learning, and colloquium.

items.title: Lab Representative
highlights: Served as the Ph.D. student representative for the SAILAB research group.
Organized internal seminars and managed communication between students and faculty.
Coordinated collaboration initiatives and lab activities.

items.title: GPU Server Management
items.focus: Linux, Docker, Git, Slurm, and research infrastructure management.
highlights: Managed GPU servers and computing resources for SAILAB's research infrastructure.
Optimized resource allocation and maintained high-performance computing environments.
Automated server monitoring and scheduling with Slurm and Docker containers.
